Name: ACM Subjects
LongName: ACM Policy for Research Involving Human Participants and Subjects
Description: Checklist for verifying ethical compliance in computing research involving human participants, including ethical review, consent, participant protection, privacy, and disclosure.
Aim: To help editors and reviewers verify that research involving human participants or identifiable participant data meets ACM's expectations for ethical oversight, informed consent, privacy protection, and transparent disclosure.

Scope.Coverage: Computing and HCI manuscripts that involve living human participants or identifiable participant data, including user studies, surveys, interviews, observational fieldwork, and experiments.
Scope.Focus: Ethical oversight, consent procedures, participant welfare, privacy and data handling, protections for vulnerable groups, and transparency about data sharing and conflicts of interest.
Scope.ContrastWith: This is an ethics-compliance guide rather than a study-design reporting guideline. It complements technical reporting guides by adding a human-subjects ethics layer for computing research.

primary.description: ACM Publications Board. Policy on Research Involving Human Participants and Subjects.

items.item_question: Does the manuscript report ethics-board approval details, or clearly justify why formal review was not required?
items.item_question_background: Human-participant research should state whether an IRB, REC, or equivalent body reviewed the study, or explain a justified exemption.
items.item_extraction_instruction: Extract the name of the ethics board, approval number, approval date, or the stated exemption rationale if formal review was not required.

items.item_question: Is the participant consent process described and appropriate for the study context?
items.item_question_background: Readers should be able to understand whether consent was written, verbal, implied, waived, or otherwise handled appropriately for the participant population.
items.item_extraction_instruction: Extract how consent was obtained, whether it was written, verbal, implied, waived, or otherwise adapted for the study.

items.item_question: Does the manuscript describe how risks to participants were minimized or managed?
items.item_question_background: Even low-risk participant studies should indicate how discomfort, burden, or potential harm was anticipated and reduced.
items.item_extraction_instruction: Extract statements about participant risks, burden, safeguards, debriefing, or other harm-reduction measures.

items.item_question: If vulnerable populations are involved, does the manuscript describe additional safeguards or justification?
items.item_question_background: Studies involving children, patients, dependent populations, or other vulnerable groups should explain extra protections.
items.item_extraction_instruction: Extract any mention of vulnerable populations and the specific added protections, permissions, or justification provided.

items.item_question: Does the manuscript explain how participant data were anonymized, secured, or otherwise protected?
items.item_question_background: Human-subjects reports should explain how participant privacy was protected, especially when collecting recordings, behavioral traces, or other identifiable data.
items.item_extraction_instruction: Extract statements about anonymization, pseudonymization, secure storage, access controls, retention, or deletion of participant data.

items.item_question: Does the manuscript state compliance with relevant privacy or data-protection requirements where applicable?
items.item_question_background: When participant data are collected, the manuscript should indicate relevant compliance frameworks if they materially shaped the study.
items.item_extraction_instruction: Extract mentions of GDPR, institutional data policies, privacy regulations, or equivalent compliance statements if present.

items.item_question: Does the manuscript state whether participant data can be shared, or explain why sharing is restricted?
items.item_question_background: Human-subjects studies should be transparent about whether data can be shared and whether privacy or consent constraints limit sharing.
items.item_extraction_instruction: Extract any data availability statement, sharing conditions, or explanation for non-sharing due to privacy, ethics, or consent restrictions.

items.item_question: Are relevant funding sources and potential conflicts of interest disclosed?
items.item_question_background: Transparent disclosure helps readers assess whether sponsor relationships or other conflicts could influence participant-facing research.
items.item_extraction_instruction: Extract funding disclosures, sponsor statements, and conflict-of-interest declarations.
items.item_expected_location: Funding / Conflicts of interest / Back matter

Aliases: ACM research involving human participants and subjects
EvidenceExamplesPositive: We conducted a user study with 24 participants after institutional review board approval and informed consent.
This HCI experiment involved human participants and includes an ethics statement and consent procedures.
EvidenceExamplesNegative: The manuscript is purely computational and does not involve any human participants or participant data.
This paper reports a systems benchmark with no user study, survey, interview, or participant-facing experiment.
This clinical manuscript reports patients and informed consent, but has no computing, software, HCI, interface, or user-study component.
Matching.Explain: Adds a supporting human-subjects ethics guide for computing manuscripts that involve participant studies or identifiable participant data.
